properties.remark: Delayed report from 1/11/24. NWS Flagstaff employee observed snow squall impacting portions of East Flagstaff. Flagstaff Airport visibility fell below 1/4 of a mile during snow squall just after 1030am MST on 1/11/24.
Wind from 315 degrees measured at 57 mph. Damage estimate $75,000+ Some rain.
Large tree limbs and branches broken in Black Canyon City. Time estimated from radar.
Trained spotted estimated a 60 mph wind gust in Black Canyon City. Damage reported to digital weather station. Time estimated from radar.
Report with photo from social media of a brief landspout tornado. Time and location estimated from radar.
Video shared of a landspout just west of Holbrook.
Half inch hail, wind gusts to 35 MPH, and heavy rain reported in Tonto Basin.
DELAYED REPORT from June 21. Strong thunderstorm winds blew roofs from structures around Immanuel Mission. Windblown hail broke windows. Time estimated from radar.
DELAYED REPORT from June 21. Large hail broke windows in and around Immanuel Mission School. Time estimated from radar.
DELAYED REPORT from June 21. Several dirt roads washed out and overtopped from flash floods in the area...locations not specific. Time estimated.
Water over Hwy 191 at Milepost 491 (near Rock Point). Stranded vehicles. Time of event estimated. Report and photo from Social Media.
DELAYED REPORT from June 22. Report from from social media. Road from Rough Rock to Blackmesa washed out, with very large boulders blocking the road. Time estimated from radar data.
Video on social media showing flooding at intersection of Butler and Foxglenn. In video a vehicle attempted to drive through the water and almost stalled.
Mesonet station AZT09 I-40 @ Two Guns MM 230.
ASOS station KINW Winslow Airport.
Thunderstorm wind gust at Winslow Airport (ASOS).
1 to 2 foot deep flash flood with debris through Antelope Canyon.
64 mph thunderstorm wind gust at Two Guns.
2.3 foot rise on Oak Creek in Sedona.
Numerous social media posts of hail covering the ground in pea to nickel size hail. One public report of quarter size hail.

A thunderstorm produced wind gust of at least 63 MPH, heavy rain (0.76 inch in 75 minutes), thunder, and lightning. Building on Moon Dr. and Highway 288 lost roof, power lines were damaged, and a hay shed was damaged.
The Winslow Airport measured a peak wind gust of 73 MPH at 1207 AM as a result of convective activity in the area. Wind gusts were above 58 MPH between 1130 PM to 1210 AM.
Estimated ping pong ball sized hail at Bullpen Day Use Area. Report from social media.
USGS stream gauge along the Agua Fria River near Humboldt (AFHA3) reported a 7.86 foot rise from upstream heavy rainfall.
Swift water rescue off of Bradshaw Mountain Road in the Quailwood Subdivision. Vehicle in the water with two occupants. Report from spotter on social media.
Post event report from Wednesday afternoon. A USGS stream gage upstream of Camp Verde (VAVA3) reported a 1 foot rise.
Post event report from Wednesday evening. A USGS stream gage downstream of Camp Verde (VCVA3) recorded a 1.68 foot rise.
USGS stream gauge along the Agua Fira River near Dewey-Humboldt (AFHA3) reported a 2.75 foot rise from nearby thunderstorms.
USGS stream gauge along the Verde River near Camp Verde (VAVA3) reported a 1.26 foot rise from earlier thunderstorms upstream.
Estimated Pine Creek running 3 feet deep. Pinewood Tavern at Hwy 87 and Hardscrabble Mesa Rd closed due to flooding.
Phone call received for trees down (approximately a foot in diameter) along HWY 87.
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd gst report from 1 ENE Prescott Airport. ASOS station KPRC Prescott Airport.
A roof was damaged and a small building was blown over a fence and destroyed in the Coyote Springs area of Prescott Valley. Time estimated from radar.
Mesonet station QHBA3 Humbug Creek.
Mesonet station QSTA3 Stanton.
Yavapai County ALERT stream gage measured a 1.5 ft rise along Big Bug Creek in Mayer.
The gage at Aqua Fria below Ash Creek (YAAA3) showed a quick, 3 foot rise. Flash flooding would have occured upstream prior to the reported rise at YAAA3.
A USGS stream gage on Tonto Creek (TNRA3) reported just over a foot rise in water level.
Photo of landspout tornado taken from 1 mile north of Snowflake. Submitter estimated the landspout to be about 10 miles to the southwest.
St. Johns police reported multiple homes being flooded along Commercial Street and along 7th Street South. Street flooding is also occurring.
A USGS stream gage along the Little Colorado River near St. Johns (LCSA3) reported a 3.8 ft rise.
A USGS river gauge on the Little Colorado River near St Johns (LCSA3) reported a 2.03 foot rise from nearby thunderstorms.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 S Uptown Sedona. A USGS stream gauge on Oak Creek in Sedona recorded a 2 foot rise in 15 minutes from upstream heavy rainfall near Munds Park.
A USGS stream gauge on Oak Creek in Sedona recorded a 2 foot rise in 15 minutes from upstream heavy rainfall near Munds Park.
Quarter sized hail (1.00 inch diameter) reported 6 miles southeast of Pine.
FB report. Golf Ball Sized hail in Crown King.
Microburst. Thunderstorm wind gust measured at Embry Riddle Aeronautical University. Power outages in area.
**CORRECTED REPORT** WET MICROBURST. THUNDERSTORM WIND MEASURED AT EMBRY RIDDLE AERONAUTICAL UNIVERSITY. POWER OUTAGES AND TREES DOWN IN THE AREA.
Heavy winds made heavy lawn furniture take flight. (Y509).
Flood in Cienega Creek of a least 3 feet.
YAAA3 Gage (Aqua Fria below Ash Creek) observed a rapid 3.5 foot rise beginning at 7:52 PM MST.
Post event report from Saturday evening. Roof shingle and window screen damage from Facebook post with video.

Post event report from Saturday 8/10. Fire department personnel reported at least 6 homes flooded in Cornville. Another Facebook video showed property and street flooding east of Page Springs Rd and north of Cornville Rd. Time of reports are estimated.
Delayed Report from Aug 10. Flooding over several roads in the Cornville area, including Cornville Rd near Cactus Rd.
Post event report from Saturday 8/10. Fire department reported flooding along Harmony, Oak Run Lane, and Page Springs Road. Flooding of multiple homes along Alvey and Brooks Lanes.
Chevelon Creek stream gauge showed a rapid rise of around 4 feet.
Reported 2 inches of rainfall in ~ 45 minutes.
**Delayed Report** Flash flood pouring into Colorado River gorge from Page area. Sourced from social media.
1.5 feet of water reported flowing in Government Tank.
The Paria River at Lees Ferry rose 1.5 feet according to a USGS stream gauge due to upstream heavy rainfall.
2 foot rise at the Lees Ferry Paria River gauge.
The Paria River at Lees Ferry rose 2.2 feet in 30 minutes with a peak flow of 1095 cfs according to a USGS stream gauge. This rise was due to earlier thunderstorms further upstream.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
The CRZA3 gauge reported a rapid 2 foot rise.

*** 1 FATAL *** Post event report from August 22. Deceased individual recovered by Grand Canyon National Park Service after being caught in a flash flood along Havasu Creek. Deceased individual was found on August 25 about 18.7 miles downstream in the Colorado River. Report time and location are approximate based on last known location.
BIA reports multiple people trapped in floodwaters. Rescues ongoing.

Gage PLFA3 at Lees Ferry showed a flash flood wave initially arrived at 02Z (1900 MST). This was due to heavy rainfall in Utah. KICX radar estimated a broad swath of 0.5 to 1.5 inches of rainfall in the Buckskin and Paria River drainages. Peak flow occurred at 05Z (2200 MST) with a stage height of 12.09 ft and 4,289 CFS. In total, PLFA3 showed a gage height rise of around 8 ft.
[Delayed Report] Over 6.5 foot rise in Granite Creek at Prescott gauge.
Weak tornado/landspout observed southwest of Tuba City near Kerley Valley verified by video.
